blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Ugh! This is ridiculous. How could this be….
Boyle was shocked to learn that Dante's mental strength had not been reduced by even one percent.
He didn't fight an archmage. He was just a classmate of the same age, and he himself was a genius who also excelled in summoning magic.
In the end, it all came down to the details.
Dante had taken control of the thousands of tiny variables that arose during the battle, widening the gap to an extreme degree.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Stop sulking and get it over with, will you? We had a deal, remember?
Dante, with his hands in his pockets, waited smugly.
It was a situation where Boyle, who was officially ranked first in the advanced class, had to kneel publicly.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: I feel a surge of anger matching the weight of my position as the top student.
But a promise was a promise, and he couldn't think of a way out.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: I… I lost.
The moment Boyle knelt down and admitted defeat, a torrential downpour poured down on the students' hearts.
Shirone and his friends, who had been cheering for Boyle, also felt heavy-hearted.
But it was a defeat without any room for excuses.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I guess we have to admit it. Information magic, that would be really tricky to deal with.
Savina intervened.

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: No, it's possible because he's Dante. We've been fighting under Teacher Olivia since we were ten. You guys haven't had nearly as much real combat experience. Dante's official record is 472 wins out of 472 battles. And in unofficial matches, he's never lost—he's a legend of undefeated battles. He's not an opponent that a mere country bumpkin academy's top student can beat.
472 official matches. If you include the unofficial ones, he's fought in over a thousand battles.
Any mage will have practical skills if he fights that much interpersonal battle.
But what made Dante truly remarkable was that he had never lost, not even once, throughout all those battles.
As Dante's background was revealed, the students began to buzz with excitement.
In an instant, he went from being a disliked classmate to a star of the kingdom.
No matter how bad his personality was, if his skill was that extraordinary, fame would follow.
No, perhaps that is why they are more enthusiastic about it.
All the students flocked to Dante.
Though they didn't show it outwardly, it was clear that they were all trying to get a word in with him.
Where the students had left like an ebb tide, only Canis and Arin remained.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: This is interesting. So magic academies train like this too.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Canis, should we give it a try?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Not really. I don't feel like it.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Aren't you afraid of losing to Dante? Hehe.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: What's the big deal? Shirone's not making a fuss, so why should I?

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Shirone's keeping quiet because he naturally dislikes fighting. You're different from him, Canis.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Arin, it seems like you've been siding with Shirone a lot lately?
Arin stuck out her tongue.
Of course, anyone who is at odds with Canis is also at odds with her.

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: Anyway, what do you think? Information magic, it seems to be a new trend. Do you think you can win?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis snorted.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: He wasn't a fan of Heaven's Trial, but Dante's skills were undeniably real.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: The interesting part was that Dante was a genius in a way that mirrored Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Iruki was also a genius, but he was a bit too one-sided in his approach.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Hmm, if I were to face him on the battlefield…

blocks.speaker: Arin
blocks.content: If you were to face him?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Canis' face, which had been silent for a long time, gradually became serious.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Sensing Canis's wavering emotions, Arin nudged him in the side, urging him on.

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: Well? If you faced him? What would happen?

blocks.speaker: Canis
blocks.content: It's, it's going to be a great match.
Match Point (1)

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Yay! I won. Hey, get on your knees, quick!

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Damn it! Let's go again! I would've won if I hadn't messed up!
At some point, a catchphrase started to spread throughout the advanced class: 'Get on your knees,' a phrase popularized by Dante.
Even in Class Seven, similar scenes were happening frequently.
Though it was just a game, the competitive atmosphere was no joke.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Tch! I don't like it. Anyway, that weird principal came in and ruined the school.
Mark, lying on the lawn, grumbled as he watched his juniors engage in hand-to-hand combat at Heaven's Trial training ground.
Currently, his match point ranked him as the top of Class Six.
He had crushed every challenger that came his way.
In the past, he would have walked around with his shoulders hunched, but after he came to respect Shirone, the ranking of the lower classes had no meaning to him.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Mark, even if the principal is annoying, you shouldn't call them weird. What if someone hears you?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: What am I supposed to do? I'm frustrated! I can't stand Dante, or whatever his name is. Especially Closer, that bastard used his passive magic when he fought me. If we fought fist to fist, he wouldn't be a match for me.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: I'm sure Senior Shirone will take care of them. After all, they're in the same Class Four. If they push you too far, I'll talk to Teacher Thad about it. What do you think?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: I'm too proud to even voice my opinion.
He wanted to get back at the guy who had bullied Maria, but he knew their skills were genuine.
Of course, as a self-proclaimed member of the 'Shirone line,' Mark had plenty of monstrous seniors like Shirone, Amy, and Iruki....

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: I really don't like this… not at all…
Since the love for battle knows no age, the students in Class Four were also keenly interested in the ranking battles.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: If Teacher Ethella and Teacher Sienna fought, who would win?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Hey, of course, it's Teacher Ethella. She's someone well-known throughout the kingdom.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: But you don't fight based on fame. Even though Teacher Ethella has achieved Triple, Teacher Sienna is an authority on freezing. In a real battle, specialists are usually the best.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you all talking about? They're all excellent teachers. They wouldn't fight each other just to prove who's stronger.
The students had indifferent expressions.
After Boyle kneeled, the center of attention in the advanced class shifted from Shirone to Dante.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: So what if we're just talking about a safe, hypothetical fight? Honestly, you're the same. You're too scared to even join the Heaven's Trial duels against Dante.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm not scared. I just don't want to fight for no reason. I don't know about tactical training, but one-on-one combat has nothing to do with our grades.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Tch, grades this, grades that. In the end, you're only good within the academy walls. A true mage should be strong in real combat.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone shrugged, realizing it was pointless to argue further.
The classmates who had placed their last hopes on provoking him turned away, disappointed when even that didn't work.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Tch, Shirone's no different after all.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: What can he do? In front of the star of the kingdom, Shirone is just a star from a small town.
Shirone couldn't figure out what the point of ranking students was.
The strength he was pursuing was different from the strength that could be achieved through fighting and subduing.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Hey, Pandora! Hurry up! We're dying of thirst here!

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Sorry, the line was too long.

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: You should've just said it was for me and cut in line. You're so inflexible.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Hehe, are you tired? Should I massage your shoulders?

blocks.speaker: Savina
blocks.content: Sure, why not? I ran too hard with Haste, and now I'm sore.
Pandora earnestly massaged Savina's shoulders.
It would be a lie to say it didn't hurt her pride, but she held onto the hope that soon they would recognize her true worth.

blocks.speaker: Closer
blocks.content: Hey, Pandora. I'm a bit thirsty too.

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, okay! I'll go get some water.
Pandora quickly ran to the makeshift water station.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Hold on. You're not seriously going to give that to him, are you?

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: What business is it of yours? Don't tell me you're holding a grudge just because you lost to Dante?

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: I've accepted my defeat. But what's up with you lately? Sure, getting close to Dante isn't bad, but do you really have to grovel like this?

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: So what does it matter to you? Why, are you anxious already? Are you afraid that I will overtake you?

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: That's not it! You've been my rival since we were kids! It just bothers me to see you bowing down like this!

blocks.speaker: Pandora
blocks.content: But that's not the case anymore. I'm joining Dante's group. Soon enough, I'll be featured in academic journals, and when I graduate, people will be lining up to scout me. Just admit you're jealous. Anything you say now lacks credibility after losing.

blocks.speaker: Boyle
blocks.content: Damn it! Do whatever you want!
Shirone's group, watching from a distance, understood how Boyle felt.
All the relationships in the advanced class now revolved around Dante.
No one dared challenge the absolute number one, and Shirone increasingly wanted to distance himself from the current situation.
He believed that if he kept refining his own abilities, success would naturally follow without needing to fight.
Day after day, Shirone dedicated himself to lifting practice, and finally, he was able to hold the target in place for a full 20 seconds.
At this point, it was practically a feat.
Maintaining a mental state that others couldn't sustain for even a second for a full 20 seconds was remarkable.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Wow, as expected from Shirone. His Spirit Zone is growing bigger and bigger. No matter what, he's an incredible talent.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Well, that's just because he's an Unlocker. He can't even look Dante in the eye.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Honestly, Dante is too strong. I understand Shirone's position as well.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Why do you assume Shirone is running away? When it comes down to it, Shirone is terrifying in competition. And don't forget, there's still Canis and Iruki.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Hmph! If he's so confident, why is he avoiding it? If I had that kind of skill, I'd challenge him out of sheer frustration. Since he's running away like that, the girls are going to go to Dante.
